items.title: Journalists given rare glimpse of Gaza City under Israel military supervision
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>With no independent access given to international media, journalists have been given a rare and limited glimpse of Gaza City under the supervision of the Israeli military.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: LIVE: Justice Department sues Ticketmaster and Live Nation
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Attorney General Merrick Garland will hold a press conference after the Justice Department filed a sweeping antitrust lawsuit against Ticketmaster and its parent company, Live Nation Entertainment. The companies are accused of running an illegal monopoly over live events in America and squelching competition.</article>

items.title: Netanyahu hopes to announce release of all hostages from Gaza 'in the coming days'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he hopes to announce the release of all hostages from Gaza “in the coming days” as indirect talks with Hamas continue in Egypt on Monday on a new U.S. plan to end the war. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Militants attack major prison in Mogadishu hours after roadblocks lifted
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Explosions and heavy gunfire erupted near Somalia’ s presidential palace on Saturday in an attack by a militant group targeting a major prison just hours after the federal government lifted roadblocks in place for years in the capital.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Brazilians change drinking habits after authorities confirm 11 methanol poisoning cases, 1 death
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A surge in methanol poisoning from adulterated alcohol has caused fear and changes in drinking habits in Sao Paulo, one death has been confirmed, and seven other cases are under investigation in Brazil. (AP Video shot by Maycron Abade. Produced by Thiago Mostazo)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Relatives of Israeli hostages in Gaza call for public to 'stand firm' over Trump peace plan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Relatives of the hostages being held by Hamas in Gaza called Saturday for the Israeli public to "stand firm" against "saboteurs" who might try to scuttle a possible deal to return them.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Some detained as pro-Palestinian protest held in London despite objections by police, politicians
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Police in London arrested several demonstrators who gathered in the city centre on Saturday to oppose the war in Gaza. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: How do giant pumpkins grow so big?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Every year, growers raise giant pumpkins and compete in annual weigh-offs against other colossal fruits. How do they get so big? (AP video/Shelby Lum)

items.title: The tomb of Amenhotep III in Luxor is opened after 20 years of renovation work
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Egypt on Saturday opened a tomb of a Pharaoh for visitors after more than two decades of renovation in the southern city of Luxor. (AP video shot by Ahmed Hatem and Amr Nabil)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Palestinians in Gaza offer cautious welcome to Hamas’ response to Trump ceasefire plan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians in the Gaza Strip on Saturday offered a cautious welcome to Hamas’ response to U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to stop the war in the coastal enclave.

items.title: Trump revives large coal sales from public lands
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The biggest government coal sale in years will soon take place. About 600 million tons will be auctioned from publicly-owned reserves next to strip mines. Data analyzed by The AP shows most of the power plants served by those mines plan to quit burning coal within 10 years. (AP Video: Mead Gruver)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Dozens injured in 'savage' Russian drone strike on Ukrainian railway station
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Dozens were injured in a “savage” Russian drone strike Saturday on a Ukrainian railway station, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as Moscow stepped up strikes on Ukraine's rail and power grids ahead of the fourth winter since its all-out invasion.  Russia struck two passenger trains in quick succession, first targeting a local service and then one bound for Kyiv, said Oleksiy Kuleba, Ukraine’s deputy prime minister.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Japan’s LDP elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM, AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Deadly floods devastate Bulgarian villages and Black Sea coast
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Damaging floods swept through villages in southeastern Bulgaria and on the Black Sea coast, claiming the lives of three people and forcing hundreds to leave their homes, authorities said on Friday. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​
